$comment: Canonical key-structure + resolution spec for all Noise Audio locale files. This file wins over any markdown mirror.

keyFormat.current: Legacy iOS keys are preserved verbatim as stable IDs (mix of snake_case, camelCase, and literal-English UI labels) so the shipping app keeps resolving. DO NOT rename an existing key without a migration.
keyFormat.goingForward: <domain>.<screen-or-element>.<variant?> — e.g. onboarding.email.title. New strings SHOULD use this dotted form.

valueShape.rule: Every key maps to an object with a required `default` and optional per-persona overrides keyed by persona ID (P1, P2, ...).

resolutionOrder: -> persona variant (if a persona is active and the variant exists)
-> key itself as last-resort literal (logged as a missing-string error)
parentIsSourceOfTruth: en is the parent. A key MUST exist in en before it can exist in any other locale. Non-parent locales omit untranslated keys and fall back to en at runtime.
addingALocale: Create src/i18n/locales/<new>/ , copy the en/*.json filenames, translate `default` values. Register the code + display name here under `locales`/`localeNames`. Missing keys fall back to en automatically.
addingAString: Add the key to the correct domain file in en/ FIRST (this defines it), then add translations to other locales as they are localized. Run scripts/i18n-check.py before committing.
escapes: \n is a hard line break inside a UI label; keep it. Acronyms (ANC, EQ, BT, OTP, TWS, IPX5, AI) stay in Roman script across all locales.
gate: No hardcoded user-facing strings in components — every string ships through a key in these files. Greppable in CI.

generatedFrom.note: English derived: literal-English keys used verbatim; slug keys auto-humanized and flagged with _todo for owner confirmation.
